commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=24748e47e352ebcd39fdcaf3f3b3ec5f6ac9327a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR moves removed-tool policy audit logging from `info` to `debug`, updates policy pipeline tests, and adds an unreleased changelog note.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +8, Docs +1. Total +9 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows doctor builds runtime tools, the shared policy pipeline audits removed tools, and current main emits those audits at `info`; the PR body also reports a Crabbox `doctor --non-interactive` run that no longer emits the normal-level audit.
commands.review_followup: - The protected `maintainer` label and the release-owned changelog edit make this a human/contributor follow-up item rather than an automatic cleanup close.

target.title: fix(doctor): quiet tool policy removal audits
target.body:
## Summary

- Move expected tool-policy removal audits from normal `info` output to `debug`.
- Keep actionable sandbox policy block audits at `info`.
- Add regression coverage that removal audits no longer hit the normal-level logger.

Fixes #87798.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-policy-pipeline.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts`
  - 45 tests passed.
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check CHANGELOG.md src/agents/tool-policy-audit.ts src/agents/tool-policy-pipeline.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/tool-policy-audit.ts src/agents/tool-policy-pipeline.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real Behavior Proof

- Provider: Crabbox AWS
- Lease: `cbx_42273b664a25`
- Slug: `jade-prawn`
- Command path: synced dirty checkout, valid isolated config with `tools.profile: "coding"`, then `node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --non-interactive`
- Success marker: `doctor_exit=0`
- Assertion: `PASS: no normal-level tool-policy removal audit emitted by doctor`
- Cleanup: released lease `cbx_42273b664a25`

## Surface Area

- No new config key, plugin contract, CLI flag, environment variable, migration, or compatibility surface.
target.author: giodl73-repo
